message:
feat(ipc): tick 158 end-to-end cx threading through client handler chain (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Ninth post-storage tick. ipc.rs had 20 `_with_cx` / 7 missing.
Filtering: 1 was #[cfg(test)]-only (`mpsc_send_value`); remaining
6 formed a coherent cx-threading chain from the per-connection
spawn point all the way down to the 3 registry-locking handlers.

New siblings, closing the chain:
- handle_pane_state_with_cx (registry_lock.read → read_with_cx)
- handle_set_pane_priority_with_cx (registry_lock.write → write_with_cx)
- handle_clear_pane_priority_with_cx (registry_lock.write → write_with_cx)
- handle_request_with_context_with_cx (routes PaneState /
  SetPanePriority / ClearPanePriority to the new cx handlers;
  UserVar/Ping/Status/Rpc delegate to the legacy dispatcher —
  Status's registry-read is telemetry-best-effort where
  cancel-surfaces-as-delay is acceptable).
- handle_client_with_context_with_cx (mirrors the legacy client
  handler body, swapping the request-dispatch call to the
  cx-first variant. IO adapters left legacy: single-line
  bounded read + single-line response write, already capped by
  MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE + 1; cx threading wouldn't add value there).

Call-site migration (L959, inside `run_with_context_with_cx`):
- Previous: `connection_tasks.spawn(async move { ... handle_client_
  with_context(stream, ctx).await ... })`
- New: clones parent cx via `let child_cx = cx.clone();`,
  spawns with `async move` capturing cx, then invokes
  `handle_client_with_context_with_cx(child_cx, stream, ctx)`.

The legacy `run_with_context` (L875) entry point is unchanged —
L894's spawn still routes to `handle_client_with_context` on the
ambient-cx path.

`recv_shutdown` (the only remaining "missing" candidate after
filtering) is a shutdown-signal probe that doesn't own a lock
acquire — its caller `run_with_context_with_cx` already pre-checks
cx via `shutdown_signal_pending_with_cx`. Leaving as legacy.

Verified via fork bypass: 98/98 ipc:: tests pass, including
`ipc_server_run_with_cx_exits_cleanly_on_shutdown` and
`ipc_pane_state_roundtrip` which transitively cover the new cx
chain from accept → spawn → handler.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
